response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, which is absent from his listed languages (English, Romanian, German), and demands deep C-for-UNIX proficiency plus Linux kernel source-modification experience — his C/C++ exposure is limited to a brief seven-month stint at National Instruments a decade ago. There is no evidence of embedded firmware, L2/L3 switch development, or security technologies such as MACsec or 802.1X.
strengths: Broad polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some systems-level work (DLang, C/C++)
Proven low-latency systems experience handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic
Strong software craftsmanship culture: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed, a hard requirement (native-level) for this role
Minimal C/UNIX experience and no Linux kernel investigation or source-modification background
No embedded networking, switch/L2-L3, or security-protocol (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal) experience

key_factors.factor: Lacks embedded C/Linux kernel and networking security experience
Strong general engineering and systems programming aptitude
